audit_items.proof_trace: anet.c contains no malloc/free calls; the only heap allocations are via getaddrinfo/freeaddrinfo. In all four deallocation sites (anetGenericResolve L226, anetTcpGenericConnect L307/L337, _anetTcpServer L499), every derived pointer (info, b, bservinfo, p/servinfo) is last-used strictly before its corresponding freeaddrinfo call, and each freeaddrinfo is called exactly once. A file-descriptor double-close exists in _anetTcpServer when anetV6Only fails (anetV6Only closes s at L459, then error: re-closes s at L496), but this is a resource-handle bug rather than a memory-pointer UAF, and is a no-op (EBADF) in Redis's single-threaded model.
